approval.note: Automate reminders and validation, not editorial acceptance. Human review remains required for Commons character, legal/privacy judgement and publication.

title: Operationalise Commonworld editorial review and freshness follow-up
goal: Turn the proposal and freshness contracts into a reviewable editorial operating loop that detects stale sources, duplicate candidates and overdue catalogue reviews without granting automation publication authority.

acceptance.assertion: Submitted, needs-information, accepted, rejected, withdrawn, published and superseded candidates remain traceable through stable status and reason codes.
The first 30-entry catalogue review is performed by 2026-10-16 and records source, action-link, activity and privacy outcomes.
Broken or stale sources become explicit editorial findings and cannot silently remain evidence for new publication claims.
Candidate review checks stable identity, title and official-domain duplication before acceptance.
Automation may prepare evidence and reminders but cannot accept or publish a candidate without review-before-effect.
